input_row.title: Ensure that network security groups are configured for Databricks subnets
input_row.description: Network Security Groups (NSGs) should be implemented to control inbound and outbound traffic to Azure Databricks subnets, ensuring only authorized communication. NSGs should be configured with deny rules to block unwanted traffic and restrict communication to essential sources only.
input_row.rationale: Impact: • NSGs require periodic maintenance to ensure rule accuracy. • Misconfigured NSGs could inadvertently block required traffic.
input_row.audit: Audit from Azure Portal 1. Navigate to Virtual Networks > Subnets, and review NSG assignments. Audit from Azure CLI az network nsg list --query "[].{Name:name, Rules:securityRules}" Audit from PowerShell Get-AzNetworkSecurityGroup -ResourceGroupName <resource-group-name>
input_row.remediation: Remediate from Azure Portal 1. Assign NSG to Databricks subnets under Networking > NSG Settings. Default Value: By default, Databricks subnets do not have NSGs assigned. References: 1. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/security/benchmark/azure/baselines/azure- databricks-security-baseline 2. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/databricks/security/network/classic/vnet- inject#network-security-group-rules
input_row.impact: • NSGs require periodic maintenance to ensure rule accuracy. • Misconfigured NSGs could inadvertently block required traffic.

gpt_response:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** azure_databricks_network_security_groups_configured

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:** 
The automated program for auditing network security groups (NSGs) configured for Azure Databricks subnets would perform the following checks:

- **Azure CLI Command**: 
  Use the following CLI command to list the NSGs and their configurations:
  ```bash
  az network nsg list --query "[].{Name:name, Rules:securityRules}" -o table
  ```

- **Resources to Enumerate**: 
  The program will target all virtual networks and their associated subnets that may include Azure Databricks resources. It should specifically review the NSGs associated with these subnets.

- **Configuration Properties/Settings to Validate**:
  1. Check for the existence of an NSG associated with each Databricks subnet.
  2. Ensure that deny rules are configured to block unwanted traffic.
  3. Validate that allow rules permit only authorized traffic sources.

- **Pass/Fail Criteria**: 
  - **Pass**: An NSG is assigned to the Databricks subnet with appropriate deny and allow rules configured per the organization's security policy.
  - **Fail**: An NSG is either not assigned or the configurations do not restrict/enforce the necessary traffic.

- **Example API Endpoint or CLI Command**: 
  An example command to check a specific NSG could be:
  ```bash
  az network nsg show --name <nsg_name> --resource-group <resource_group_name>
  ```

This would further detail the specific security rules within the queried NSG.
